commands.author: steipete
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8bb3b739c06696e833d75ed41dc4e04c19ed1ae1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the bundled session-memory transcript reader to skip delivery-mirror assistant entries and collapse consecutive duplicate assistant text, with regression tests for the linked duplication report.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +82. Total +101 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main extracts text from consecutive assistant rows, so raw-thinking plus cleaned-text rows with the same visible text produce duplicate `assistant:` lines. I did not run a live thinking-enabled model flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The branch needs contributor or maintainer revision plus real behavior proof before merge; automated repair should not substitute for the missing external setup proof.

target.title: fix: clean up Kiro CLI helper processes
target.body:
## Summary

- launch Kiro CLI commands with a dedicated process group established before `exec`
- terminate residual helpers after normal parent exit, escalating from `TERM` to `KILL` before draining inherited pipes
- preserve existing timeout, cancellation, output, and exit-status behavior
- strengthen the inherited-pipe regression to require cleanup of a helper that ignores graceful termination

Follow-up to #1533 and #1535.

## Root Cause

The merged fix bounded pipe draining, but its best-effort `setpgid` call happened after `Foundation.Process.run()`. On macOS the child can cross `exec` before that call, causing `setpgid` to fail with `EACCES`. A normally exiting Kiro parent could therefore return while a pipe-holding helper survived outside any controllable process group.

This change uses `posix_spawn` with `POSIX_SPAWN_SETPGROUP`, matching the repository's existing pre-exec process-group pattern.

## Proof

Exact head: `aa59361ab0188c2d4ea45cc1469902808f4f1010`

- `swift test --filter KiroStatusProbeTests --skip-update`: 31 passed
- `swift test --filter SubprocessRunnerTests --skip-update`: 8 passed
- `make test`: 469 deterministic selections across 40 shards passed
- `make check`: parser/package/release guards passed; SwiftFormat clean; SwiftLint 0 violations
- `swift build -c release --product CodexBarCLI`: passed
- release CLI synthetic Kiro proof: parent exited normally, descendant retained the output pipe and ignored `HUP`/`TERM`, command returned successfully in 0.79s, descendant was terminated
- local autoreview: no accepted or actionable findings
- Public Model Identifier Gate: PASS; no model identifiers added to source, tests, artifacts, logs, or public proof

The runtime proof used an isolated temporary home and synthetic account data. No live credentials, browser data, Keychain access, private payloads, or user-identifying paths were used.

## Risk

The new process launcher is package-internal and currently used only by Kiro. macOS behavior is covered locally; CI validates the conditional Linux build.
target.author: steipete
